questions.stem: An entity holds a beneficial interest in a securitized pool of mortgage loans. The beneficial interest is classified as a held-to-maturity debt security. Under ASC 326, how should the entity measure expected credit losses on this beneficial interest?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Expected credit losses are measured based on the underlying mortgage loans in the securitization pool

choices.label: B
choices.text: Expected credit losses are measured on the beneficial interest as a unit of account, considering the structure and credit enhancement of the securitization

choices.label: C
choices.text: Beneficial interests in securitizations are exempt from CECL measurement

choices.label: D
choices.text: Expected credit losses are measured at zero because the securitization structure provides sufficient credit protection

questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-20-30-11, for beneficial interests that are not PCD assets, the entity measures expected credit losses on the beneficial interest itself (the unit of account held by the investor), considering the structure of the securitization, including subordination, overcollateralization, and other credit enhancements. The entity does not look through to the underlying assets (A is incorrect). Beneficial interests in securitizations are within the scope of CECL (C is incorrect). The securitization structure does not automatically result in zero losses (D is incorrect). The answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-30-11により、PCD資産でない受益権については、証券化のストラクチャー（劣後、超過担保、信用補完等）を考慮して受益権自体（投資家が保有する会計単位）で期待信用損失を測定する。基礎となるモーゲージローンを見通して測定するのではない。証券化の受益権はCECLの範囲から免除されない。また、証券化ストラクチャーがあるからといって自動的にゼロ損失にはならない。正解はB。

questions.stem: An entity previously determined that it did not intend to sell an AFS debt security with a credit loss allowance of $25,000 and a non-credit loss in OCI of $40,000. At the current reporting date, the entity now intends to sell the security. The fair value is $435,000 and the amortized cost is $500,000. What journal entry should the entity record upon changing its intent?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No entry is required until the security is actually sold

choices.label: B
choices.text: Write off the entire amortized cost basis and recognize a $500,000 loss

choices.label: C
choices.text: Increase the allowance to equal the full difference between amortized cost and fair value ($65,000), with the additional $40,000 charged to credit loss expense, and reclassify the non-credit loss from OCI to credit loss expense

choices.label: D
choices.text: Reverse the existing $25,000 allowance to income and record the full $65,000 decline in OCI

questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-30-35-10, when an entity intends to sell or more likely than not will be required to sell an AFS debt security before recovery of its amortized cost basis, the entity must write down the security to fair value by recording the entire difference between amortized cost and fair value through the allowance. The allowance must equal the full decline: $500,000 − $435,000 = $65,000. The existing allowance is $25,000, so an additional $40,000 must be recognized through credit loss expense. The non-credit loss previously in OCI ($40,000) is reclassified. The answer is C.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-30-35-10により、AFS債務証券を売却する意図がある場合、償却原価と公正価値の全差額を引当金で反映する。引当金 = $500,000 − $435,000 = $65,000。既存引当金$25,000に対し追加$40,000を信用損失費用で認識。OCIの非信用損失は組替。正解はC。

questions.stem: After the adoption of ASU 2022-02, how should a creditor account for a loan modification that would have previously been classified as a troubled debt restructuring (TDR)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The creditor must still separately identify TDRs and apply the pre-ASU 2022-02 concession-based measurement guidance

choices.label: B
choices.text: The creditor evaluates whether the modification represents a new loan or a continuation of the existing loan, and measures expected credit losses under the general CECL model without a separate TDR designation

choices.label: C
choices.text: The creditor writes off the entire loan balance upon modification and recognizes a new loan at fair value

choices.label: D
choices.text: The creditor records the modification as a gain or loss in net income equal to the difference between the old and new contractual terms

questions.explanation: ASU 2022-02 (codified in ASC 326-20-35-10A through 10C) eliminated the TDR designation and its related measurement guidance for entities that have adopted CECL. Instead, creditors evaluate loan modifications under general loan modification guidance — determining whether the modification is a new loan or a continuation of an existing loan. Expected credit losses continue to be measured under the CECL model. Enhanced disclosures about modifications to borrowers experiencing financial difficulty are required in place of the former TDR disclosures. Separately identifying TDRs and applying the pre-ASU 2022-02 concession-based measurement guidance is incorrect, as the TDR designation was eliminated. Writing off the entire loan balance and recognizing a new loan at fair value, or recording a gain or loss equal to the difference between the old and new contractual terms, do not reflect the post-ASU 2022-02 guidance. The answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2022-02（ASC 326-20-35-10Aから10Cに成文化）はCECL採用企業についてTDR指定と関連する測定ガイダンスを廃止した。代わりに、貸手は一般的なローン修正ガイダンスに基づき修正が新規ローンか既存ローンの継続かを評価し、期待信用損失は引き続きCECLモデルで測定する。旧TDR開示に代わり、財務困難を経験している借り手への修正に関する強化開示が必要。TDRを引き続き個別に識別し旧来の譲歩ベースの測定ガイダンスを適用するのは、TDR指定が廃止されたため不正確。修正時にローン全額を償却して公正価値で新規ローンを認識する処理や、新旧契約条件の差額を損益計上する処理は、ASU 2022-02後のガイダンスを反映していない。正解はB。

questions.stem: Under ASC 326-20-50, which of the following components must be presented in the required allowance for credit losses rollforward disclosure?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Beginning balance, provision for credit losses, write-offs, recoveries, and ending balance

choices.label: B
choices.text: Beginning balance, fair value adjustments, and ending balance only

choices.label: C
choices.text: Total credit loss expense for the period without further disaggregation

choices.label: D
choices.text: Ending allowance balance and weighted-average loss rate only

questions.explanation: ASC 326-20-50-13 requires a rollforward schedule of the allowance for credit losses showing the beginning balance, current-period provision (credit loss expense), write-offs charged against the allowance, recoveries of amounts previously written off, and the ending balance. This rollforward must be presented by portfolio segment. Fair value adjustments are not part of the allowance rollforward. Presenting only total credit loss expense without disaggregation, or only the ending allowance balance and a weighted-average loss rate, provides insufficient detail compared to what is required. The answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-50-13は、引当金のロールフォワードスケジュールとして期首残高、当期繰入額（信用損失費用）、引当金から控除された償却、過去の償却の回収、期末残高の開示を要求。このロールフォワードはポートフォリオセグメント別に提示する必要がある。公正価値調整は引当金ロールフォワードの一部ではない。当期の信用損失費用合計のみの開示や、期末残高と加重平均損失率のみの開示は、要求される詳細度に比べ不十分。正解はA。

questions.stem: Lakeside Bank holds a commercial loan portfolio with an exposure at default of $8,000,000. Management estimates a probability of default of 2.5% and a loss given default of 55%. Exposure at default equals the carrying amount. Under ASC 326, what allowance for expected credit losses should be recorded?
questions.explanation: Expected credit loss = EAD × PD × LGD = $8,000,000 × 2.5% × 55% = $110,000. CECL requires recognition of lifetime expected credit losses at the reporting date based on the loss-rate components. Applying only the 2.5% default probability and ignoring loss given default overstates the loss to $200,000. Applying the 55% severity to the full exposure while ignoring the 2.5% default probability produces $440,000. Doubling the 2.5% rate to 5.0% on the full exposure produces $220,000.
questions.explanationJa: 期待信用損失 = EAD × PD × LGD = $8,000,000 × 2.5% × 55% = $110,000。CECLでは報告日時点で存続期間の期待信用損失を認識する。LGDを無視し2.5%のみを適用すると$200,000、PDを無視し55%のみを全エクスポージャーに適用すると$440,000、2.5%を5.0%に倍化すると$220,000となる誤りである。

questions.stem: Meridian Finance began the year with an allowance for credit losses of $60,000. During the year it charged off $18,000 of uncollectible loans and recovered $5,000 previously written off. The required year-end allowance is $72,000. What is the provision for credit losses recognized in the income statement?
questions.explanation: Allowance rollforward: Beginning $60,000 − charge-offs $18,000 + recoveries $5,000 + provision = ending $72,000. Provision = $72,000 − $60,000 + $18,000 − $5,000 = $25,000. The provision is the amount needed to bring the allowance to the required balance after net charge-offs. The net increase in the allowance alone is $12,000. Ignoring the $5,000 of recoveries yields $30,000. Adding recoveries instead of subtracting them yields $35,000.

questions.stem: Coastal Supply Co. groups its $4,500,000 of trade receivables into three risk tiers: Tier 1 ($2,500,000, loss rate 1%), Tier 2 ($1,500,000, loss rate 3%), and Tier 3 ($500,000, loss rate 12%). Using the loss-rate method, what is the total allowance for expected credit losses?
questions.explanation: Tier 1: $2,500,000 × 1% = $25,000. Tier 2: $1,500,000 × 3% = $45,000. Tier 3: $500,000 × 12% = $60,000. Total = $25,000 + $45,000 + $60,000 = $130,000. Applying a flat 3% to the whole balance gives $135,000. Omitting Tier 3 gives $70,000. Applying a flat 2% to the total gives $90,000.

questions.stem: Harbor Lending originated a vintage of installment loans with an original balance of $1,000,000. Lifetime loss rates by year are: Year 1 = 1%, Year 2 = 2.5%, Year 3 = 1.5%, Year 4 = 0.5%. By the end of Year 2, $20,000 of losses on this vintage have already been incurred and charged off against the allowance. Under the vintage method, what allowance is required at the end of Year 2 for the remaining expected losses over the rest of the vintage's life?
questions.explanation: Because the Year 1 and Year 2 losses have already been incurred and charged off, the allowance at the end of Year 2 covers only the expected losses remaining in Years 3 and 4. Remaining loss rate = 1.5% + 0.5% = 2.0%, so the required allowance = $1,000,000 × 2.0% = $20,000. The full lifetime loss rate of 5.5% produces $55,000, the entire lifetime figure. Subtracting the $20,000 already charged off from the $55,000 lifetime figure produces $35,000 and double-counts the realized losses. Applying a 3.0% rate to the remaining periods produces $30,000.
questions.explanationJa: Year 1・Year 2の損失は既に発生し引当金から償却済みのため、Year 2末の引当金はYear 3・Year 4に残る期待損失のみを対象とする。残存損失率 = 1.5% + 0.5% = 2.0%、必要引当金 = $1,000,000 × 2.0% = $20,000。生涯損失率5.5%では$55,000（生涯全額）、生涯$55,000から償却済み$20,000を控除すると$35,000（実現損失の二重計上）、残存期間に3.0%を適用すると$30,000となる。

questions.stem: Summit Bank holds a $700,000 loan with an effective interest rate of 6%. After a credit event, management estimates the present value of expected future cash flows, discounted at the loan's original effective rate of 6%, is $612,000. The fair value of the loan is $590,000. Under the DCF method of ASC 326-20, what is the expected credit loss?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-20-30-4, when an entity measures expected credit losses using a DCF method, the loss equals amortized cost less the present value of expected cash flows discounted at the loan's effective interest rate: $700,000 − $612,000 = $88,000. Fair value of $590,000 is not used in the DCF approach. Using fair value instead of the present value of cash flows ($700,000 − $590,000) gives $110,000. The gap between the present value and fair value ($612,000 − $590,000) is $22,000. Applying a flat 10% to the loan gives $70,000.
questions.explanationJa: DCF法では、信用損失 = 償却原価 − 実効金利で割引いた期待キャッシュフローの現在価値 = $700,000 − $612,000 = $88,000。公正価値$590,000はDCF法では使用しない。公正価値を誤用すると（$700,000 − $590,000）$110,000、現在価値と公正価値の差は$22,000、10%を適用すると$70,000となる。

questions.stem: Granite Bank holds a $900,000 loan to a borrower experiencing financial difficulty. Repayment is expected to be provided substantially through the sale of the underlying real estate collateral, and the bank elects the collateral-dependent practical expedient. The collateral has a fair value of $740,000, and the bank expects to incur selling costs of $44,000. What expected credit loss should the bank record?
questions.explanation: When the entity expects to sell the collateral, expected credit loss = amortized cost − (fair value of collateral − costs to sell) = $900,000 − ($740,000 − $44,000) = $900,000 − $696,000 = $204,000. Omitting selling costs ($900,000 − $740,000) gives $160,000. Subtracting selling costs from the unadjusted shortfall ($160,000 − $44,000) gives $116,000. The selling costs alone are $44,000.
questions.explanationJa: 担保を売却する見込みの場合、期待信用損失 = 償却原価 − (担保の公正価値 − 売却費用) = $900,000 − ($740,000 − $44,000) = $900,000 − $696,000 = $204,000。売却費用を無視すると$160,000、未調整の不足額から売却費用を控除すると$116,000、売却費用のみは$44,000である。

questions.stem: Pioneer Bank holds an $850,000 loan to a borrower in financial difficulty and applies the collateral-dependent practical expedient. The bank intends to operate, not sell, the collateral; its fair value is $620,000 and estimated costs to sell are $40,000. What expected credit loss should be recorded?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-20-35-5, when repayment is expected through operation (not sale) of the collateral, costs to sell are excluded. Expected credit loss = amortized cost − fair value of collateral = $850,000 − $620,000 = $230,000. Improperly subtracting selling costs ($850,000 − ($620,000 − $40,000)) gives $270,000. Improperly adding selling costs to the collateral value ($850,000 − ($620,000 + $40,000)) gives $190,000. The selling costs alone are $40,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-35-5では、担保の運用（売却ではなく）を通じた回収が見込まれる場合、売却費用は控除しない。期待信用損失 = 償却原価 − 担保の公正価値 = $850,000 − $620,000 = $230,000。誤って売却費用を控除すると$270,000、誤って売却費用を担保価値に加算すると$190,000、売却費用のみは$40,000である。

questions.subtopic: AFS Debt Securities - Credit vs Non-Credit Split
questions.stem: Westfield Corp. holds an AFS corporate bond with an amortized cost of $600,000 and a fair value of $520,000. It does not intend to sell and will not be required to sell the security. The present value of expected cash flows, discounted at the bond's effective rate, is $555,000. How much of the $80,000 decline is recognized as a credit loss in earnings?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-30, the credit loss equals amortized cost minus the present value of expected cash flows = $600,000 − $555,000 = $45,000, recorded through an allowance in earnings. The non-credit portion is $80,000 − $45,000 = $35,000, recognized in OCI. The credit loss is capped at the total decline of $80,000, but $45,000 is below that cap. The entire decline of $80,000 wrongly treats all of the loss as credit-related. The non-credit OCI portion is $35,000. A credit loss of $25,000 understates the shortfall by stopping below the full amortized cost versus present value difference.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-30では信用損失 = 償却原価 − 期待キャッシュフローの現在価値 = $600,000 − $555,000 = $45,000を引当金経由で損益認識する。非信用部分は$80,000 − $45,000 = $35,000でOCI計上。信用損失の上限は下落総額$80,000だが$45,000はそれ未満。下落総額$80,000は全額を信用損失とする誤り、非信用OCI部分は$35,000、$25,000は償却原価と現在価値の差に届かず不足額を過少評価したものである。

questions.subtopic: AFS Debt Securities - Allowance Capped at Decline
questions.stem: Brookline Bank holds an AFS Treasury-backed security with an amortized cost of $500,000 and a fair value of $470,000. The present value of expected cash flows discounted at the effective rate is $455,000. The bank neither intends nor is required to sell. What credit-loss allowance is recognized in earnings?
questions.explanation: The measured credit loss based on cash flows is amortized cost − PV of expected cash flows = $500,000 − $455,000 = $45,000. Under ASC 326-30-35-4, the AFS credit-loss allowance is limited to the amount by which fair value is below amortized cost = $500,000 − $470,000 = $30,000. The allowance recognized is the lesser amount, $30,000. The cash-flow shortfall of $45,000 ignores the fair-value cap. The gap between fair value and the present value of cash flows is $15,000. A $0 allowance incorrectly assumes no credit loss exists.
questions.explanationJa: キャッシュフローに基づく信用損失 = 償却原価 − 期待キャッシュフローのPV = $500,000 − $455,000 = $45,000。ASC 326-30-35-4により、AFSの信用損失引当金は公正価値が償却原価を下回る額（$500,000 − $470,000 = $30,000）を上限とする。認識額は小さい方の$30,000。キャッシュフロー不足$45,000は上限を無視、公正価値とPVの差は$15,000、$0は信用損失なしと誤認したものである。

questions.subtopic: HTM Debt Securities - Net Carrying Amount
questions.stem: Ridgeway Inc. classifies a debt security as held-to-maturity with an amortized cost of $3,200,000. The estimated lifetime expected credit loss is $140,000, and the security's fair value is $3,050,000. Under ASC 326-20, at what net carrying amount is the HTM security reported on the balance sheet?
questions.explanation: HTM securities are reported at amortized cost less the allowance for credit losses, regardless of fair value. Net carrying amount = $3,200,000 − $140,000 = $3,060,000. Fair value of $3,050,000 is disclosed but not used for measurement on the balance sheet. Reporting at fair value of $3,050,000 ignores the measurement rule. Reporting at $3,200,000 ignores the allowance. Subtracting the allowance from fair value ($3,050,000 − $140,000) gives $2,910,000.
questions.explanationJa: HTM有価証券は公正価値にかかわらず償却原価から信用損失引当金を控除して報告する。純帳簿価額 = $3,200,000 − $140,000 = $3,060,000。公正価値$3,050,000は開示されるが測定には使用しない。公正価値$3,050,000での計上は測定ルールを無視、$3,200,000は引当金を無視、公正価値から引当金を控除すると$2,910,000となる。

questions.stem: Anchor Bank purchases a pool of distressed loans for $1,100,000. The contractual unpaid principal balance is $1,500,000. At acquisition, the estimated lifetime expected credit loss is $250,000. Under the PCD model in ASC 326-20, what is the initial amortized cost basis of the loans (the gross-up basis)?
questions.explanation: For PCD assets, the day-one allowance is added to the purchase price to establish the amortized cost basis, with no credit-loss expense at acquisition. Amortized cost = purchase price + initial allowance = $1,100,000 + $250,000 = $1,350,000. The remaining non-credit discount = $1,500,000 − $1,350,000 = $150,000 is accreted to interest income over the life. The unpaid principal balance is $1,500,000. The purchase price alone is $1,100,000. Subtracting the allowance from the purchase price ($1,100,000 − $250,000) gives $850,000.
questions.explanationJa: PCD資産では、当初引当金を購入価格に加算して償却原価を設定し、取得時に信用損失費用は計上しない。償却原価 = 購入価格$1,100,000 + 当初引当金$250,000 = $1,350,000。非信用ディスカウント$1,500,000 − $1,350,000 = $150,000は存続期間にわたり利息収益として加算する。未払元本は$1,500,000、購入価格のみは$1,100,000、購入価格から引当金を控除すると$850,000となる。

questions.stem: Sterling Bank purchases a pool of performing loans (not credit-deteriorated) for $980,000, equal to their amortized cost basis. At the acquisition date, the bank estimates a lifetime expected credit loss of $35,000 on the pool. How does Sterling record the day-one allowance?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $35,000 credit-loss expense in the income statement, with the loan basis remaining $980,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: $35,000 added to the loan basis, with no credit-loss expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: No allowance is recorded until a loss is incurred

choices.label: D
choices.text: $35,000 recognized directly in OCI

questions.explanation: For purchased financial assets that are NOT credit-deteriorated (non-PCD), the gross-up approach does not apply. The entity establishes an allowance through a charge to credit-loss expense at acquisition, just like an originated asset, and the loan basis stays at $980,000. Adding the allowance to basis with no expense is the PCD treatment, which does not apply to non-PCD assets. CECL requires day-one recognition rather than deferring until an incurred loss arises, and credit losses on amortized-cost assets flow through earnings, not OCI.
questions.explanationJa: 信用劣化していない購入金融資産（非PCD）にはグロスアップ法は適用されない。組成資産と同様に取得時に信用損失費用を計上して引当金を設定し、ローン基礎は$980,000のまま。費用なしで基礎に加算するのはPCD処理であり非PCDには当てはまらない。CECLは発生損失を待たず初日認識を要求し、償却原価資産の信用損失はOCIではなく損益を通す。

questions.stem: Anchorage Bank enters into a reverse repurchase agreement that is continuously secured by collateral the counterparty is contractually required to replenish so that the collateral's fair value always at least equals the amortized cost basis of the asset. The bank reasonably expects the counterparty to continue replenishing the collateral. Under the collateral maintenance practical expedient in ASC 326-20, how may the bank measure expected credit losses on this asset?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It may estimate expected credit losses as zero to the extent the fair value of the collateral equals or exceeds the amortized cost basis and the counterparty is expected to continue replenishing the collateral

choices.label: B
choices.text: It must measure a lifetime expected credit loss using a probability-of-default model regardless of the collateral arrangement

choices.label: C
choices.text: It must write the asset down to the fair value of the collateral at each reporting date

choices.label: D
choices.text: It is exempt from the CECL model entirely because the asset is fully collateralized

questions.explanation: ASC 326-20-35-6 provides a collateral maintenance practical expedient for financial assets secured by collateral that the borrower is required to continually adjust so its fair value at least equals the amortized cost basis. When the entity reasonably expects the borrower to continue replenishing the collateral, expected credit losses may be estimated as zero to the extent the collateral's fair value equals or exceeds the amortized cost basis. A full probability-of-default measurement is not required when this expedient applies. The asset is not written down to collateral fair value, and a fully collateralized asset remains within the CECL model even though the measured loss may be zero.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-35-6は、借り手が担保の公正価値を償却原価以上に維持するよう継続的に調整することを求められる金融資産に対し、担保維持の実務上の簡便法を定める。借り手が担保を補充し続けると合理的に見込まれる場合、担保の公正価値が償却原価以上である範囲で期待信用損失をゼロと見積もることができる。この簡便法が適用される場合、PDモデルによる全面測定は不要である。資産を担保公正価値まで切り下げることはなく、全額担保された資産も測定損失がゼロであってもCECLの範囲内に留まる。

questions.stem: Cedar Credit Union estimates losses on a $6,000,000 loan pool using the weighted-average remaining maturity (WARM) method. The pool has a weighted-average remaining maturity of 5 years and an average annual charge-off rate of 0.4%. Assuming no adjustment for prepayments, what is the expected credit loss?
questions.explanation: Under the WARM method, the expected loss = balance × annual loss rate × weighted-average remaining maturity = $6,000,000 × 0.4% × 5 = $120,000. WARM applies the average annual charge-off rate over the remaining life of the pool. Using a single year of loss without the remaining maturity gives $24,000. Using a 1% annual rate gives $300,000. Using a 0.2% annual rate over five years gives $60,000.
questions.explanationJa: WARM法では、期待損失 = 残高 × 年間損失率 × 加重平均残存期間 = $6,000,000 × 0.4% × 5 = $120,000。WARMは平均年間償却率をプールの残存期間にわたって適用する。残存期間を考慮せず1年分のみでは$24,000、1%を適用すると$300,000、0.2%を5年適用すると$60,000となる。

questions.stem: Fairmont Bank has an unconditionally cancellable line-of-credit commitment with an undrawn amount of $2,000,000. Management expects $1,200,000 of the commitment to be funded over its life and applies a lifetime loss rate of 3% to the funded exposure. Under ASC 326-20, what amount should be recognized as a liability for the off-balance-sheet credit exposure?
questions.explanation: ASC 326-20-30-11 requires an allowance for expected credit losses on off-balance-sheet credit exposures only for the period the entity is exposed to credit risk through a present contractual obligation that is NOT unconditionally cancellable. Because this commitment is unconditionally cancellable by the bank, no liability is recorded. Applying 3% to the full undrawn amount gives $60,000, applying 3% to the $1,200,000 expected funding gives $36,000, and applying 3% to $800,000 gives $24,000, but each wrongly accrues a loss despite the unconditional cancellability.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-30-11は、無条件で取消可能でない現在の契約義務を通じて信用リスクに晒される期間についてのみ、オフバランス信用エクスポージャーの引当を要求する。本コミットメントは銀行が無条件で取消可能なため負債は計上しない。未実行額全額に3%で$60,000、見込実行$1,200,000に3%で$36,000、$800,000に3%で$24,000となるが、いずれも取消可能性を無視した誤りである。

questions.stem: Belmont Bank has issued a non-cancellable loan commitment with an undrawn balance of $1,500,000. Management estimates that $900,000 will ultimately be funded and applies a lifetime expected loss rate of 4% to the amount expected to be funded. What liability for the unfunded commitment should be recorded under ASC 326-20?
questions.explanation: Because the commitment is non-cancellable, the bank is exposed to credit risk and must record a liability based on the amount expected to be funded over the commitment's life: $900,000 × 4% = $36,000. The estimate considers the likelihood that funding will occur. Applying the rate to the full undrawn balance ($1,500,000 × 4%) gives $60,000. Applying the rate to $600,000 gives $24,000. Recording nothing wrongly assumes no accrual is required.
questions.explanationJa: コミットメントが取消不能なため、銀行は信用リスクに晒され、存続期間にわたり実行が見込まれる額に基づき負債を計上する：$900,000 × 4% = $36,000。実行の可能性を考慮する。未実行残高全額に適用すると$60,000、$600,000に適用すると$24,000、計上しないのは引当不要との誤認である。

questions.stem: On adoption of ASC 326, Tidewater Bank's incurred-loss allowance was $400,000 and its CECL day-one allowance is $640,000. The applicable tax rate is 21%. Using the modified-retrospective transition method, what is the net-of-tax decrease to opening retained earnings?
questions.explanation: Increase in allowance = $640,000 − $400,000 = $240,000 (pretax). Deferred tax benefit = $240,000 × 21% = $50,400, recorded as an increase in the deferred tax asset. Net-of-tax charge to retained earnings = $240,000 − $50,400 = $189,600. The pretax amount alone is $240,000. The tax effect alone is $50,400. Applying a 44% rate to the increase gives $134,400.

questions.stem: Linden Wholesale estimates credit losses on trade receivables using an aging schedule with the following balances and historical loss rates: Current $1,500,000 (0.5%), 1-30 days $400,000 (2%), 31-60 days $150,000 (8%), and over 60 days $100,000 (25%). What total allowance for credit losses should be recognized?

Ashford Bank's historical lifetime loss rate on a $5,000,000 commercial pool is 2%. Considering current and forecasted economic conditions (rising unemployment in its region), management increases the rate by a qualitative adjustment of 60 basis points. What is the adjusted allowance for expected credit losses?
questions.explanation: CECL requires adjusting historical loss experience for current conditions and reasonable and supportable forecasts. Adjusted rate = 2.0% + 0.60% = 2.60%. Allowance = $5,000,000 × 2.60% = $130,000. The unadjusted historical allowance at 2.0% is $100,000. The incremental qualitative adjustment alone (0.60%) is $30,000. Applying a 3.2% rate gives $160,000.
questions.explanationJa: CECLは過去の損失実績を現在の状況と合理的で裏付け可能な予測に基づき調整する。調整後率 = 2.0% + 0.60% = 2.60%。引当金 = $5,000,000 × 2.60% = $130,000。未調整の過去引当金（2.0%）は$100,000、質的調整増分のみ（0.60%）は$30,000、3.2%を適用すると$160,000となる。

questions.stem: Crestview Bank estimates credit losses on a $4,000,000 pool using three probability-weighted scenarios: baseline (loss rate 2%, weight 50%), adverse (loss rate 5%, weight 30%), and severely adverse (loss rate 9%, weight 20%). What is the probability-weighted expected credit loss?
questions.explanation: Weighted loss rate = (2% × 0.50) + (5% × 0.30) + (9% × 0.20) = 1.0% + 1.5% + 1.8% = 4.3%. Expected loss = $4,000,000 × 4.3% = $172,000. Applying a flat 4% rate to the pool gives $160,000. Applying the severely-adverse 9% rate alone gives $360,000. Simple-averaging the three rates (5.333%) instead of probability-weighting them gives $213,333.

questions.stem: Hartwell Bank holds $2,000,000 of U.S. Treasury securities classified as held-to-maturity. The securities are explicitly backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. government and have a long history of zero credit losses with no expectation of nonpayment. What allowance for credit losses should the bank record under ASC 326-20?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $0, because the expectation of nonpayment is zero

choices.label: B
choices.text: A nominal 0.01% allowance is still required for all financial assets

choices.label: C
choices.text: An allowance equal to the difference between cost and fair value

choices.label: D
choices.text: An allowance based on the entity's overall portfolio loss rate

questions.explanation: ASC 326-20-30-10 permits an entity to record zero expected credit losses when the expectation of nonpayment is zero, such as for U.S. Treasury securities backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. government with a long history of no credit losses. CECL does not mandate a floor allowance on every asset, fair-value declines are not the credit-loss measure for HTM securities, and a portfolio-wide rate would not reflect these instruments' specific zero-loss characteristics.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-30-10は、米国の完全な信用で裏付けられ信用損失の長い無実績を持つ米国財務省証券など、不払いの予想がゼロの場合に信用損失をゼロとすることを認める。CECLは全資産に最低引当を課さず、公正価値下落はHTMの信用損失尺度ではなく、ポートフォリオ全体率はこれら特有のゼロ損失性を反映しない。

questions.subtopic: AFS Debt Securities - Intent to Sell
questions.stem: Northgate Corp. holds an AFS debt security with an amortized cost of $700,000 and a fair value of $610,000. At the reporting date, management intends to sell the security before recovery of its amortized cost. How should the $90,000 decline be accounted for under ASC 326-30?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $90,000 is recorded in OCI as an unrealized loss

choices.label: B
choices.text: The credit portion is recorded in earnings and the remainder in OCI

choices.label: C
choices.text: The amortized cost is written down to $610,000 with the entire $90,000 recognized in earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: No loss is recognized until the security is actually sold

questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-30-35-10, when an entity intends to sell an AFS security (or more likely than not will be required to sell before recovery), it writes down the amortized cost basis to fair value, and the entire difference ($700,000 − $610,000 = $90,000) is recognized in earnings. The credit-versus-noncredit allowance split does not apply once there is intent to sell, and deferring the loss to OCI or until disposal would be inappropriate.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-30-35-10では、AFS証券を売却する意図がある（または回収前に売却が要求される可能性が高い）場合、償却原価を公正価値まで切り下げ、差額全額（$700,000 − $610,000 = $90,000）を損益認識する。売却意図がある場合、信用・非信用の引当分割は適用されず、OCIや処分時までの繰延は不適切である。

questions.stem: At year-end, Glenwood Bank reduced its CECL allowance from $310,000 to $250,000 because forecasted economic conditions improved. There were no charge-offs or recoveries during the period. How should the $60,000 change be reflected in the income statement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a $60,000 reduction of credit-loss expense (a credit/negative provision)

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a $60,000 gain reported in other comprehensive income

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a direct increase to retained earnings, bypassing the income statement

choices.label: D
choices.text: No adjustment, because reversals of credit-loss allowances are prohibited

questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-20-35-1, changes in the allowance for expected credit losses, including decreases due to improved forecasts, are recognized in the income statement as a credit-loss expense (or a reversal/benefit). The $60,000 reduction lowers credit-loss expense for the period. CECL allowance reversals flow through earnings, not OCI or directly to equity, and reversals are required when expectations improve, not prohibited.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-35-1では、予測改善による減少を含む期待信用損失引当金の変動は、損益計算書で信用損失費用（または戻入・便益）として認識する。$60,000の減少は当期の信用損失費用を減らす。CECLの戻入はOCIや資本直接ではなく損益を通し、期待改善時は戻入が要求され禁止されない。

questions.stem: Riverton Bank acquired a PCD loan with a contractual unpaid principal balance of $1,000,000 for a purchase price of $760,000. The day-one expected credit-loss allowance is $180,000. The non-credit discount is accreted to interest income using the effective-interest method over an estimated 4-year life on a straight-line approximation. Ignoring credit-allowance changes, what amount is accreted to interest income in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Amortized cost (gross-up basis) = purchase price + day-one allowance = $760,000 + $180,000 = $940,000. Non-credit discount = unpaid principal − amortized cost = $1,000,000 − $940,000 = $60,000. Accreted straight-line over 4 years: $60,000 ÷ 4 = $15,000 per year. Spreading the full $180,000 allowance over 4 years gives $45,000. Accreting the entire $60,000 non-credit discount in one year gives $60,000. Combining the discount with the allowance ($60,000 + $180,000 ÷ ...) overstates the amount to $190,000.
questions.explanationJa: 償却原価（グロスアップ基礎） = 購入価格$760,000 + 当初引当金$180,000 = $940,000。非信用ディスカウント = 未払元本$1,000,000 − 償却原価$940,000 = $60,000。4年で直線加算：$60,000 ÷ 4 = 年$15,000。引当金$180,000全額を4年配分すると$45,000、1年で全額加算すると$60,000、ディスカウントと引当金を混同すると$190,000となる。

questions.subtopic: Expected Recoveries in the Allowance Estimate
questions.stem: Westbrook Bank estimates gross lifetime expected credit losses of $500,000 on a loan pool. Based on collateral and historical collection experience, the bank also expects to recover $80,000 of amounts within that pool that would otherwise be written off. The expected recoveries do not exceed the amounts written off and expected to be written off. Under ASC 326-20, what net allowance for credit losses should the bank record on this pool?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-20-30-1, the allowance for credit losses is a valuation account that reflects expected credit losses net of expected recoveries of amounts previously written off or expected to be written off, provided those expected recoveries do not exceed the amounts written off and expected to be written off. Net allowance = gross expected losses − expected recoveries = $500,000 − $80,000 = $420,000. Ignoring expected recoveries leaves $500,000. Adding expected recoveries to gross losses gives $580,000. The expected recoveries alone are $80,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-30-1により、信用損失引当金は、既に償却したまたは償却が見込まれる金額の期待回収額（その回収額が償却済みおよび償却見込額を超えない範囲）を控除した期待信用損失を反映する評価勘定である。純引当金 = 総期待損失 − 期待回収額 = $500,000 − $80,000 = $420,000。期待回収を無視すると$500,000、総損失に加算すると$580,000、期待回収のみは$80,000である。

questions.stem: A bank reports four financial assets. Three of them are measured at amortized cost and one is carried at fair value through net income. Applying the scope guidance in ASC 326-20, the bank must record a CECL allowance on three of these assets but is not required to do so on the fourth. On which asset is a CECL allowance NOT required?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Held-to-maturity debt securities measured at amortized cost

choices.label: B
choices.text: A loan receivable measured at amortized cost

choices.label: C
choices.text: A loan measured at fair value through net income

choices.label: D
choices.text: A net investment in a sales-type lease

questions.explanation: The CECL model applies to financial assets measured at amortized cost. A loan measured at fair value through net income is excluded because changes in its value (including credit deterioration) are already captured in earnings through fair value remeasurement, so a separate allowance would be redundant. Held-to-maturity securities, amortized-cost loans, and net investments in leases are all within CECL's scope.
questions.explanationJa: CECLモデルは償却原価で測定される金融資産に適用される。公正価値を通じて純損益で測定されるローンは、信用劣化を含む価値変動が公正価値再測定により既に損益に反映されるため除外され、別途の引当は重複となる。HTM証券、償却原価ローン、正味リース投資はすべてCECLの範囲内である。

questions.subtopic: Allowance Limited to Amortized Cost Basis
questions.stem: Fenwick Bank holds an individually evaluated loan with an amortized cost basis of $200,000 to a severely distressed borrower. Its credit-loss model, based on expected cash flows, indicates expected credit losses of $250,000. Under ASC 326-20, what allowance for credit losses should the bank record on this loan?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-20-30-1, the allowance for credit losses is a valuation account deducted from the amortized cost basis to present the net amount expected to be collected. Because the net carrying amount cannot be reduced below zero, the allowance is limited to the amortized cost basis of $200,000, even though the model indicates a larger shortfall. The uncapped model output is $250,000. The excess of the model output over the amortized cost basis is $50,000. Recording $0 ignores the substantial expected loss entirely.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-30-1により、信用損失引当金は回収見込純額を表示するため償却原価から控除される評価勘定である。純帳簿価額をゼロ未満にはできないため、モデルがより大きな不足を示しても引当金は償却原価$200,000を上限とする。上限を設けないモデル値は$250,000、モデル値が償却原価を超える額は$50,000、$0は多額の期待損失を全く無視したものである。

questions.subtopic: Loss Rate Method - Current Conditions Adjustment
questions.stem: Orchard Bank segments its $3,000,000 loan portfolio into two pools. Pool 1 ($1,800,000) has a historical lifetime loss rate of 2%, which management adjusts upward by 0.5 percentage points to reflect deteriorating regional employment conditions affecting only that pool. Pool 2 ($1,200,000) has a historical lifetime loss rate of 4% with no adjustment needed. What is the total allowance for credit losses under ASC 326?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-20, historical loss rates must be adjusted for current conditions and reasonable and supportable forecasts. Pool 1: $1,800,000 × (2% + 0.5%) = $45,000. Pool 2: $1,200,000 × 4% = $48,000. Total allowance = $45,000 + $48,000 = $93,000. An answer of $84,000 results from using unadjusted historical rates for both pools, ignoring the required current-conditions adjustment. An answer of $99,000 results from incorrectly applying the 0.5% adjustment to both pools rather than only Pool 1. An answer of $75,000 results from applying a single 2.5% rate to the entire $3,000,000 portfolio.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20では、過去の損失率を現在の状況および合理的で裏付け可能な予測に基づいて調整する必要があります。プール1: $1,800,000 × (2% + 0.5%) = $45,000。プール2: $1,200,000 × 4% = $48,000。合計引当金 = $93,000。$84,000は調整を無視して過去実績率のみを使用した誤り、$99,000は0.5%の調整をプール1だけでなく両プールに適用した誤り、$75,000はポートフォリオ全体$3,000,000に単一の2.5%を適用した誤りです。

questions.stem: Birchwood Finance began the year with an allowance for credit losses of $80,000. During the year, the company recorded a provision for credit losses of $30,000, wrote off $25,000 of uncollectible receivables, and collected $5,000 on accounts previously written off. What is the ending balance of the allowance for credit losses?
questions.explanation: The allowance rollforward is: beginning balance + provision − write-offs + recoveries = ending balance. $80,000 + $30,000 − $25,000 + $5,000 = $90,000. Recoveries of previously written-off amounts are credited back to the allowance, not recognized as income. An answer of $85,000 results from ignoring the $5,000 recovery. An answer of $80,000 results from incorrectly subtracting the recovery instead of adding it. An answer of $140,000 results from adding the write-offs instead of subtracting them.
questions.explanationJa: 引当金のロールフォワードは「期首残高 + 繰入額 − 償却額 + 回収額 = 期末残高」です（ASC 326-20）。$80,000 + $30,000 − $25,000 + $5,000 = $90,000。償却済債権の回収は収益ではなく引当金に戻し入れます。$85,000は回収$5,000を無視した誤り、$80,000は回収を加算ではなく減算した誤り、$140,000は償却額を減算ではなく加算した誤りです。

questions.stem: Kingsley Bank's allowance for credit losses had a beginning balance of $120,000 and an ending balance of $140,000. During the year, the bank recorded a provision for credit losses of $95,000 and recoveries of previously written-off loans of $15,000. What amount of gross write-offs did the bank record during the year?
questions.explanation: Rearranging the rollforward equation: write-offs = beginning balance + provision + recoveries − ending balance = $120,000 + $95,000 + $15,000 − $140,000 = $90,000. An answer of $75,000 results from omitting the $15,000 of recoveries. An answer of $60,000 results from subtracting recoveries instead of adding them. An answer of $105,000 results from a computational error that double-counts a portion of the recoveries.
questions.explanationJa: ロールフォワードの式を変形すると、償却額 = 期首残高 + 繰入額 + 回収額 − 期末残高 = $120,000 + $95,000 + $15,000 − $140,000 = $90,000。$75,000は回収$15,000を省いた誤り、$60,000は回収を加算ではなく減算した誤り、$105,000は回収を二重に計算した誤りです（ASC 326-20-50-13の引当金増減表開示の構成要素）。

questions.subtopic: PCD Assets - Non-Credit Discount at Acquisition
questions.stem: Dorset Bank purchases a pool of loans that qualifies as purchased credit-deteriorated (PCD) for $640,000. The contractual unpaid principal balance is $800,000, and the Day 1 allowance for credit losses is determined to be $90,000. What amount of non-credit discount will be accreted into interest income over the life of the loans?
questions.explanation: Under the PCD gross-up approach, the initial amortized cost basis equals the purchase price plus the Day 1 allowance: $640,000 + $90,000 = $730,000. The non-credit discount is the difference between the unpaid principal balance and the initial amortized cost: $800,000 − $730,000 = $70,000. Only this non-credit discount is accreted into interest income; the $90,000 credit discount becomes the allowance and is never accreted. An answer of $160,000 represents the total discount ($800,000 − $640,000), which incorrectly includes the credit portion. An answer of $90,000 is the credit discount, which is recorded as the allowance, not accreted. An answer of $0 incorrectly assumes no discount accretion occurs for PCD assets.
questions.explanationJa: PCD資産のグロスアップ方式（ASC 326-20-30-13）では、当初償却原価 = 購入価格 + Day 1引当金 = $640,000 + $90,000 = $730,000。非信用ディスカウント = 未払元本残高 − 当初償却原価 = $800,000 − $730,000 = $70,000。利息収益として実効金利法で取り崩されるのはこの非信用部分のみで、信用ディスカウント$90,000は引当金として計上され取り崩されません。$160,000はディスカウント総額で信用部分を誤って含んでいます。$90,000は引当金となる信用ディスカウントです。

questions.subtopic: Discounted Cash Flow Method - Single Payment
questions.stem: Maple Bank holds a loan with an amortized cost basis of $300,000 and an effective interest rate of 10%. Following the borrower's financial deterioration, the bank expects to collect a single payment of $242,000 one year from the reporting date. Using the discounted cash flow method under ASC 326, what allowance for credit losses should the bank record?
questions.explanation: When using the DCF method, expected cash flows are discounted at the financial asset's effective interest rate. Present value = $242,000 ÷ 1.10 = $220,000. Allowance = amortized cost − present value of expected cash flows = $300,000 − $220,000 = $80,000. An answer of $58,000 results from comparing the undiscounted expected cash flow ($242,000) to amortized cost. An answer of $22,000 is merely the discount effect ($242,000 − $220,000). An answer of $0 incorrectly assumes no allowance is needed because some payment is expected.
questions.explanationJa: DCF法（ASC 326-20-30-4）では、期待キャッシュフローを当該金融資産の実効金利で割り引きます。現在価値 = $242,000 ÷ 1.10 = $220,000。引当金 = 償却原価 − 期待キャッシュフローの現在価値 = $300,000 − $220,000 = $80,000。$58,000は割引前のキャッシュフロー$242,000と償却原価を比較した誤り、$22,000は割引効果のみの金額、$0は一部回収が見込まれることを理由に引当不要とした誤りです。

questions.subtopic: Aging Method - Provision with Existing Allowance
questions.stem: Larkspur Distributing estimates expected credit losses on trade receivables using an aging schedule: current $500,000 at 1%; 1–30 days past due $200,000 at 5%; 31–60 days past due $100,000 at 10%; over 60 days past due $50,000 at 40%. The allowance for credit losses has an existing credit balance of $12,000 before adjustment. What provision for credit losses should be recognized for the period?
questions.explanation: Required allowance = ($500,000 × 1%) + ($200,000 × 5%) + ($100,000 × 10%) + ($50,000 × 40%) = $5,000 + $10,000 + $10,000 + $20,000 = $45,000. Because the allowance already has a $12,000 credit balance, the provision is $45,000 − $12,000 = $33,000. An answer of $45,000 is the total required allowance, not the period provision. An answer of $57,000 results from adding the existing balance instead of subtracting it. An answer of $12,000 is simply the existing balance.

questions.stem: Telford Bank holds a $500,000 loan to a borrower experiencing financial difficulty. Repayment is expected to be provided substantially through the sale of the collateral, and the bank elects the collateral-dependent practical expedient. The collateral has a fair value of $560,000, and estimated costs to sell are $40,000. What allowance for credit losses should the bank record on this loan?
questions.explanation: Under the collateral-dependent practical expedient, when repayment is expected through sale of the collateral, the allowance is measured as the amortized cost basis less the fair value of collateral minus costs to sell. Fair value less costs to sell = $560,000 − $40,000 = $520,000, which exceeds the $500,000 amortized cost basis. Because the net collateral value fully covers the loan, no allowance is required. An answer of $40,000 incorrectly records the costs to sell as the allowance. An answer of $20,000 incorrectly records the excess of net collateral value over the loan as a loss. An answer of $60,000 incorrectly treats the gross excess of collateral fair value over the loan balance as an allowance.
questions.explanationJa: 担保依存の実務上の便法（ASC 326-20-35-4）では、担保売却による回収が見込まれる場合、引当金 = 償却原価 − (担保の公正価値 − 売却費用)。$560,000 − $40,000 = $520,000 が償却原価$500,000を上回るため、引当金は$0です。$40,000は売却費用をそのまま引当金とした誤り、$20,000は担保純額の超過分を損失として計上した誤り、$60,000は担保公正価値の超過総額を引当金とした誤りです。

questions.subtopic: HTM Debt Securities - Expected Cash Flows vs Fair Value
questions.stem: Quarry Bank holds a held-to-maturity corporate bond with an amortized cost basis of $2,400,000. A discounted cash flow analysis indicates the present value of expected cash flows is $2,250,000. The bond's current fair value is $2,180,000. What provision for credit losses should the bank record, assuming no prior allowance exists?
questions.explanation: HTM debt securities are within the scope of ASC 326-20, which measures expected credit losses based on expected cash flows, not fair value. The allowance = amortized cost − present value of expected cash flows = $2,400,000 − $2,250,000 = $150,000. Unlike the AFS model in ASC 326-30, there is no fair value floor or cap for HTM securities. An answer of $220,000 incorrectly uses the fair value decline ($2,400,000 − $2,180,000). An answer of $70,000 is the difference between the PV of expected cash flows and fair value, which is not relevant to the HTM measurement. An answer of $0 incorrectly assumes HTM securities are exempt from credit loss recognition.
questions.explanationJa: HTM負債証券はASC 326-20（償却原価モデル）の適用範囲であり、期待信用損失は公正価値ではなく期待キャッシュフローに基づいて測定します。引当金 = $2,400,000 − $2,250,000 = $150,000。AFSモデル（ASC 326-30）と異なり、HTMには公正価値による上限・下限はありません。$220,000は公正価値の下落額を使用した誤り、$70,000は期待CF現在価値と公正価値の差で測定に無関係、$0はHTM証券が信用損失認識の対象外とした誤りです。

questions.subtopic: AFS Debt Securities - Amount Recognized in OCI
questions.stem: Halverson Corp. holds an available-for-sale debt security with an amortized cost of $900,000 and a fair value of $810,000. Halverson does not intend to sell the security and will not more likely than not be required to sell it. A discounted cash flow analysis indicates the present value of cash flows expected to be collected is $850,000. What amount of the decline in fair value is recognized in other comprehensive income?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-30, the credit loss portion is recognized in earnings through an allowance: amortized cost − PV of expected cash flows = $900,000 − $850,000 = $50,000. The non-credit portion of the fair value decline is recognized in OCI: total decline $90,000 − credit loss $50,000 = $40,000. An answer of $90,000 incorrectly puts the entire decline in OCI, ignoring the credit component. An answer of $50,000 is the credit loss recognized in earnings, not OCI. An answer of $0 incorrectly assumes the entire decline goes to earnings.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-30では、信用損失部分は引当金を通じて損益に計上します: 償却原価 − 期待CF現在価値 = $900,000 − $850,000 = $50,000。公正価値下落のうち非信用部分はOCIに計上します: 下落総額$90,000 − 信用損失$50,000 = $40,000。$90,000は下落額全額をOCIとし信用部分を無視した誤り、$50,000は損益計上される信用損失額、$0は全額を損益とした誤りです。

questions.subtopic: AFS Debt Securities - Intent to Sell with Existing Allowance
questions.stem: Marbury Inc. holds an available-for-sale debt security with an amortized cost of $700,000 and a fair value of $620,000. In a prior period, Marbury recognized an allowance for credit losses of $30,000 on this security. At the current reporting date, Marbury decides to sell the security. What additional charge to earnings should Marbury recognize in the current period?
questions.explanation: When an entity intends to sell an impaired AFS debt security, ASC 326-30-35-10 requires the allowance to be written off and the security's amortized cost basis to be written down to fair value through earnings. The total cumulative charge needed is $700,000 − $620,000 = $80,000. Because $30,000 was already recognized through the allowance in a prior period, the additional current-period charge is $80,000 − $30,000 = $50,000. An answer of $80,000 ignores the previously recognized allowance. An answer of $30,000 is the prior allowance, not the incremental charge. An answer of $110,000 incorrectly adds the prior allowance to the total decline.
questions.explanationJa: AFS負債証券の売却を意図する場合、ASC 326-30-35-10により引当金を取り崩し、償却原価を公正価値まで損益を通じて切り下げます。必要な累計損失 = $700,000 − $620,000 = $80,000。前期に$30,000が引当金として既に認識済みのため、当期の追加損失 = $80,000 − $30,000 = $50,000。$80,000は既認識の引当金を無視した誤り、$30,000は前期の引当額そのもの、$110,000は下落総額に前期引当額を加算した誤りです。

questions.stem: Compton Bank has issued a non-cancellable loan commitment with an undrawn amount of $2,000,000. The bank estimates a 60% likelihood that the commitment will be funded, and the expected loss rate on funded amounts is 3%. What liability for off-balance-sheet credit exposure should the bank recognize under ASC 326?
questions.explanation: For off-balance-sheet credit exposures that are not unconditionally cancellable, the entity estimates expected credit losses on the portion expected to be funded: $2,000,000 × 60% × 3% = $36,000, recognized as a liability (not as a contra-asset allowance). An answer of $60,000 ignores the probability of funding ($2,000,000 × 3%). An answer of $0 incorrectly treats the commitment as exempt; only unconditionally cancellable commitments are excluded. An answer of $120,000 results from incorrectly applying a 6% combined rate to the full commitment.
questions.explanationJa: 無条件に取消可能でないオフバランス信用エクスポージャーについては、実行が見込まれる部分の期待信用損失を見積もり負債として計上します（ASC 326-20-30-11）: $2,000,000 × 60% × 3% = $36,000。これは資産の控除項目ではなく負債として表示します。$60,000は実行確率60%を無視した誤り、$0は無条件取消可能な場合にのみ適用される除外を誤適用、$120,000は6%を全額に適用した誤りです。

questions.subtopic: Accrued Interest Receivable - Write-Off by Reversing Interest Income
questions.stem: Ferndale Bank places a commercial loan on nonaccrual status. The loan has accrued interest receivable of $9,000. Ferndale has elected the policy under ASC 326 to write off uncollectible accrued interest receivable by reversing interest income, and it excludes accrued interest from its allowance measurement. What is the effect of writing off the $9,000 accrued interest?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Credit loss expense increases by $9,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: The allowance for credit losses decreases by $9,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Other comprehensive income decreases by $9,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Interest income decreases by $9,000 with no effect on credit loss expense

questions.explanation: ASC 326-20-30-5A permits an entity that writes off uncollectible accrued interest receivable in a timely manner to elect not to measure an allowance on accrued interest, and to write it off by reversing interest income. Under this election, the $9,000 write-off reduces interest income directly and bypasses both credit loss expense and the allowance. Recording credit loss expense would apply only if the entity had elected to write off accrued interest through the allowance. OCI is not affected because this is an amortized cost asset, not an AFS fair value adjustment.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-30-5Aにより、回収不能な未収利息を適時に償却する企業は、未収利息に引当金を設定せず、利息収益の戻し入れにより償却する方針を選択できます。この選択の下では、$9,000の償却は利息収益を直接減額し、信用損失費用にも引当金にも影響しません。信用損失費用の計上は引当金経由の償却を選択した場合のみです。償却原価測定資産のためOCIにも影響しません。

questions.subtopic: Transition - Cumulative-Effect Adjustment Net of Tax
questions.stem: On January 1, Whitfield Bank adopts ASC 326 using the modified retrospective approach. Its allowance under the prior incurred loss model was $250,000, and the allowance required under CECL at the adoption date is $330,000. Whitfield's tax rate is 25%. What is the effect of adoption on beginning retained earnings?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Decrease of $80,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Decrease of $60,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Decrease of $20,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No effect; the $80,000 increase in the allowance is charged to current-period earnings

questions.explanation: ASC 326 is adopted through a cumulative-effect adjustment to beginning retained earnings, not through current-period earnings. The allowance increases by $330,000 − $250,000 = $80,000, which also creates a deferred tax asset of $80,000 × 25% = $20,000. The net retained earnings decrease is $80,000 − $20,000 = $60,000. A decrease of $80,000 ignores the tax effect. A decrease of $20,000 is only the deferred tax asset. Charging the adjustment to current-period earnings contradicts the modified retrospective transition method.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326の適用は修正遡及アプローチにより期首利益剰余金への累積影響額の調整で行い、当期損益には計上しません（ASC 326-10-65-1）。引当金の増加額 = $330,000 − $250,000 = $80,000。これにより繰延税金資産 $80,000 × 25% = $20,000 が生じるため、利益剰余金の純減少額 = $80,000 − $20,000 = $60,000。$80,000の減少は税効果を無視した誤り、$20,000は繰延税金資産のみの金額です。

questions.stem: Stanton Bank's commercial loan portfolio totals $10,000,000. One loan of $1,000,000 no longer shares similar risk characteristics with the rest of the portfolio and is evaluated individually, resulting in an allowance of $150,000. The remaining loans are evaluated collectively using a 2% expected loss rate. What is the total allowance for credit losses?
questions.explanation: ASC 326-20-30-2 requires collective (pool) evaluation when assets share similar risk characteristics; an asset that does not share those characteristics is evaluated individually and removed from the pool. Collective portion: ($10,000,000 − $1,000,000) × 2% = $180,000. Individual portion: $150,000. Total = $330,000. An answer of $350,000 results from applying the 2% rate to the full $10,000,000 without removing the individually evaluated loan, double-counting it. An answer of $200,000 applies 2% to the whole portfolio and ignores the individual evaluation. An answer of $180,000 omits the individually evaluated loan's allowance.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-30-2では、類似のリスク特性を共有する資産はプール単位で評価し、共有しない資産はプールから除外して個別評価します。プール部分: ($10,000,000 − $1,000,000) × 2% = $180,000。個別評価部分: $150,000。合計 = $330,000。$350,000は個別評価対象の貸付金をプールから除外せず二重計上した誤り、$200,000は全体に2%を適用し個別評価を無視した誤り、$180,000は個別評価分を omit した誤りです。

questions.stem: Glenrock Bank originates a credit facility consisting of a funded loan balance of $3,000,000 and a non-cancellable unfunded commitment of $1,000,000. The expected loss rate is 2%, and the bank expects 50% of the unfunded commitment to be drawn. What total credit loss expense should be recognized at origination, and how is the unfunded portion presented?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $60,000; the entire amount is presented as a contra-asset allowance

choices.label: B
choices.text: $80,000; $20,000 of which is presented as a liability

choices.label: C
choices.text: $70,000; $10,000 of which is presented as a liability

choices.label: D
choices.text: $10,000; presented entirely as a liability

questions.explanation: Funded portion: $3,000,000 × 2% = $60,000, recognized as an allowance (contra-asset). Unfunded portion: $1,000,000 × 50% expected funding × 2% = $10,000, recognized as a separate liability for off-balance-sheet credit exposure. Total credit loss expense = $60,000 + $10,000 = $70,000. An answer of $60,000 omits the unfunded commitment exposure. An answer of $80,000 with a $20,000 liability ignores the 50% expected funding assumption. An answer of $10,000 omits the funded loan's allowance.
questions.explanationJa: 実行済部分: $3,000,000 × 2% = $60,000（資産の控除項目である引当金）。未実行部分: $1,000,000 × 実行見込み50% × 2% = $10,000（オフバランス信用エクスポージャーに係る独立した負債、ASC 326-20-30-11）。信用損失費用合計 = $70,000。$60,000は未実行コミットメントを無視、$80,000（うち負債$20,000）は実行見込み50%を無視、$10,000は実行済貸付金の引当金を無視した誤りです。

questions.subtopic: Trade Receivables and Contract Assets - Combined Estimate
questions.stem: Brightline Services reports trade accounts receivable of $600,000 and contract assets under ASC 606 of $400,000. Expected loss rates are 2% for receivables and 1.5% for contract assets. What total allowance for credit losses should Brightline record under ASC 326?
questions.explanation: Both trade receivables and contract assets arising from ASC 606 are within the scope of CECL. Receivables: $600,000 × 2% = $12,000. Contract assets: $400,000 × 1.5% = $6,000. Total allowance = $18,000. An answer of $12,000 incorrectly excludes contract assets from the CECL scope. An answer of $6,000 covers only the contract assets. An answer of $20,000 results from applying the 2% rate to the combined $1,000,000 balance.
questions.explanationJa: 売掛金とASC 606に基づく契約資産はいずれもCECLの適用範囲です（ASC 326-20-15-2）。売掛金: $600,000 × 2% = $12,000。契約資産: $400,000 × 1.5% = $6,000。引当金合計 = $18,000。$12,000は契約資産をCECLの範囲から誤って除外、$6,000は契約資産のみ、$20,000は合計$1,000,000に2%を適用した誤りです。

questions.subtopic: Static Pool Analysis - Applying Historical Lifetime Rate
questions.stem: Rockwell Lending analyzes a closed (static) pool of loans originated in a prior year with an original balance of $5,000,000, which experienced cumulative lifetime losses of $200,000. Management applies this historical lifetime loss rate to its current $6,000,000 pool of similar loans, adjusted upward by 0.5 percentage points for a weaker economic forecast. What is the allowance for credit losses?
questions.explanation: The historical lifetime loss rate from the static pool is $200,000 ÷ $5,000,000 = 4%. Adjusted for the forecast: 4% + 0.5% = 4.5%. Allowance = $6,000,000 × 4.5% = $270,000. An answer of $240,000 results from applying the unadjusted 4% rate, ignoring the required forecast adjustment. An answer of $200,000 simply repeats the historical dollar losses without scaling to the current pool. An answer of $225,000 results from applying the adjusted 4.5% rate to the original $5,000,000 pool instead of the current pool.
questions.explanationJa: 静的プールの過去のライフタイム損失率 = $200,000 ÷ $5,000,000 = 4%。予測調整後: 4% + 0.5% = 4.5%。引当金 = $6,000,000 × 4.5% = $270,000（ASC 326-20-30-9、過去実績は現在の状況と合理的で裏付け可能な予測で調整）。$240,000は予測調整を無視して4%を適用した誤り、$200,000は過去の損失額をそのまま使用した誤り、$225,000は調整後4.5%を現在のプールではなく元の$5,000,000に適用した誤りです。

questions.subtopic: AFS Debt Securities - Partial Reversal of Allowance
questions.stem: In a prior year, Lockhart Corp. recognized an allowance for credit losses of $60,000 on an available-for-sale debt security it does not intend to sell. At the current reporting date, an updated discounted cash flow analysis indicates the credit loss is now $35,000, and the security's fair value remains below amortized cost by more than that amount. How should Lockhart account for the change?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Reverse $25,000 of the allowance through credit loss expense (a benefit) in earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: No adjustment; reversals of AFS credit loss allowances are prohibited

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reverse the entire $60,000 allowance through earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize a $35,000 increase in other comprehensive income

questions.explanation: Under ASC 326-30-35-3, the allowance approach permits improvements in expected credit losses on AFS debt securities to be reversed through earnings immediately. The allowance is reduced from $60,000 to $35,000, producing a $25,000 reversal recognized as a benefit in credit loss expense. Prohibiting reversals describes the legacy other-than-temporary impairment model, which ASC 326 replaced specifically to allow such reversals. Reversing the full $60,000 ignores the remaining $35,000 credit loss. Recording the change in OCI confuses the credit component (earnings) with the non-credit component (OCI).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-30-35-3の引当金アプローチでは、AFS負債証券の期待信用損失の改善は直ちに損益を通じて戻し入れます。引当金を$60,000から$35,000に減額し、$25,000を信用損失費用の戻入益として認識します。戻入禁止は旧OTTI（一時的でない減損）モデルの考え方であり、ASC 326はまさに戻入を可能にするために引当金方式を採用しました。$60,000全額の戻入は残存する信用損失$35,000を無視しています。OCI計上は信用部分（損益）と非信用部分（OCI)の混同です。

questions.stem: Under ASC 326, which of the following purchased financial assets should be classified as purchased credit-deteriorated (PCD)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Any financial asset purchased at a discount to its par value

choices.label: B
choices.text: Any purchased financial asset whose fair value is below its contractual principal balance

choices.label: C
choices.text: Any purchased financial asset that is past due by at least one day at the acquisition date

choices.label: D
choices.text: A purchased financial asset that, as of the acquisition date, has experienced a more-than-insignificant deterioration in credit quality since origination

questions.explanation: ASC 326-20-20 defines PCD assets as acquired individual financial assets (or groups with similar risk characteristics) that, as of the date of acquisition, have experienced a more-than-insignificant deterioration in credit quality since origination, as determined by the acquirer's assessment. A purchase discount alone does not make an asset PCD, because discounts can reflect interest rate changes rather than credit deterioration. Fair value below contractual principal similarly may result from market interest rates. Past-due status is an indicator to consider but is not by itself determinative.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-20は、PCD資産を「取得日時点で、組成時から信用度が軽微とはいえない程度に悪化している取得金融資産」と定義しています。ディスカウント購入だけではPCDになりません。ディスカウントは金利変動を反映している可能性があるためです。公正価値が契約元本を下回ることも市場金利が原因の場合があります。延滞状況は考慮すべき指標の一つですが、それ単独では決定要因になりません。

questions.subtopic: CECL Scope - Operating Lease Receivables Excluded
questions.stem: Which of the following receivables is excluded from the scope of the CECL model under ASC 326-20?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Receivables arising from operating leases

choices.label: B
choices.text: Trade accounts receivable arising from revenue transactions

choices.label: C
choices.text: A lessor's net investment in a sales-type lease

choices.label: D
choices.text: Contract assets recognized under ASC 606

questions.explanation: ASC 326-20-15-3 explicitly excludes receivables arising from operating leases; their collectibility is assessed under ASC 842, which addresses operating lease income recognition. In contrast, trade receivables, contract assets under ASC 606, and a lessor's net investment in sales-type and direct financing leases are all within the CECL scope and require an allowance for expected credit losses.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-15-3は、オペレーティング・リースから生じる債権をCECLの適用範囲から明示的に除外しています。その回収可能性はASC 842（リース）の枠組みで評価されます。一方、売掛金、ASC 606に基づく契約資産、貸手の販売型リース・直接金融リースに係る正味リース投資はいずれもCECLの適用範囲であり、期待信用損失の引当金が必要です。

questions.stem: A bank's reasonable and supportable forecast period covers two years, but its loan pool has a seven-year remaining life. Regarding the reversion to historical loss information for periods beyond the forecast horizon, which statement is most accurate under ASC 326?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The bank must extend its forecast to cover the full seven-year life of the pool

choices.label: B
choices.text: The bank must revert to historical loss information on a straight-line basis over the remaining life

choices.label: C
choices.text: The bank may revert immediately or gradually; ASC 326 does not prescribe a specific reversion technique

choices.label: D
choices.text: The bank may ignore periods beyond the forecast horizon and measure losses only for the two-year forecast period

questions.explanation: ASC 326-20-30-9 requires reversion to historical loss information for periods beyond which the entity can develop reasonable and supportable forecasts, but it does not prescribe how to revert. An entity may revert immediately, on a straight-line basis, or using another systematic rational method. Requiring a forecast over the entire asset life contradicts the standard, which acknowledges forecasting ability diminishes over time. Mandating straight-line reversion overstates the requirement. Measuring losses only for the forecast period violates the lifetime loss principle of CECL.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-30-9は、合理的で裏付け可能な予測ができない期間については過去の損失情報への回帰（reversion）を求めていますが、回帰の方法は規定していません。即時回帰、定額的な段階回帰、その他の規則的で合理的な方法のいずれも認められます。資産の全期間にわたる予測の強制は基準の趣旨に反し、定額法の強制は要求の過大解釈です。予測期間のみの損失測定はCECLのライフタイム損失原則に違反します。

questions.subtopic: Vintage Disclosure - Credit Quality by Year of Origination
questions.stem: Under ASC 326-20-50, which disclosure regarding credit quality is required for a public business entity's financing receivables?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The fair value of each financing receivable class, disaggregated by industry of the borrower

choices.label: B
choices.text: The amortized cost basis by credit quality indicator, disaggregated by year of origination (vintage)

choices.label: C
choices.text: The names of the ten largest borrowers and their outstanding balances

choices.label: D
choices.text: A sensitivity analysis showing the effect of a one-percentage-point change in assumed loss rates

questions.explanation: ASC 326-20-50-6 requires public business entities to disclose the amortized cost basis of financing receivables and net investments in leases by credit quality indicator, disaggregated by year of origination (vintage disclosure), generally for the five most recent annual periods plus prior years in aggregate. Fair value by borrower industry, identification of the largest borrowers, and loss-rate sensitivity analyses are not required credit quality disclosures under ASC 326.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-50-6は、公開営利企業（PBE）に対し、融資債権および正味リース投資の償却原価を信用度指標別かつ組成年度別（ヴィンテージ）に開示することを要求しています。通常、直近5年度分を年度別に、それ以前は一括して表示します。借手の業種別公正価値、大口借手の氏名、損失率の感応度分析はASC 326で要求される信用度開示ではありません。

questions.subtopic: PCD vs Non-PCD - Acquisition Accounting Difference
questions.stem: Two banks each acquire a pool of loans and each determines that expected credit losses exist at the acquisition date. Bank X's pool qualifies as purchased credit-deteriorated (PCD); Bank Y's pool does not. What is the key difference in accounting for expected credit losses at acquisition?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Bank X recognizes credit loss expense at acquisition; Bank Y adds the allowance to the purchase price

choices.label: B
choices.text: Neither bank records an allowance until losses are incurred

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both banks recognize credit loss expense at acquisition for the full allowance

choices.label: D
choices.text: Bank X adds the Day 1 allowance to the purchase price with no income statement effect; Bank Y recognizes the Day 1 allowance through credit loss expense

questions.explanation: For PCD assets, the Day 1 allowance is added to the purchase price under the gross-up approach (ASC 326-20-30-13), so no credit loss expense is recognized at acquisition. For non-PCD purchased assets, the entity records the asset at its purchase price and establishes the Day 1 allowance through a charge to credit loss expense, the same as for originated assets. The reversed combination misstates both treatments. Deferring recognition until losses are incurred describes the superseded incurred loss model. Recognizing expense for both ignores the PCD gross-up mechanism.
questions.explanationJa: PCD資産では、グロスアップ方式（ASC 326-20-30-13）によりDay 1引当金を購入価格に加算して当初償却原価を決定するため、取得時に信用損失費用は計上されません。非PCDの購入資産では、購入価格で資産を計上し、Day 1引当金を信用損失費用への繰入により設定します（組成した資産と同じ処理）。「損失発生まで引当金を計上しない」は廃止された発生損失モデルの記述です。両方とも費用認識とするのはPCDのグロスアップを無視しています。

questions.stem: Which statement best describes the estimation methods permitted for measuring expected credit losses under ASC 326-20?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Entities must use a discounted cash flow method for all financial assets

choices.label: B
choices.text: Entities must use the method prescribed by their primary banking regulator

choices.label: C
choices.text: No specific method is required; loss-rate, roll-rate, aging, discounted cash flow, and probability-of-default methods are all acceptable

choices.label: D
choices.text: Public business entities must use a probability-of-default method, while other entities may use a loss-rate method

questions.explanation: ASC 326-20-30-3 states that the standard does not require a specific method for estimating expected credit losses. Entities may use loss-rate methods, roll-rate methods, aging schedules, discounted cash flow analyses, probability-of-default methods, or other reasonable approaches appropriate to the assets. A DCF method is one acceptable choice, not a mandate. Banking regulators do not dictate the U.S. GAAP estimation method. There is no method requirement that differs between public business entities and other entities.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-30-3は、期待信用損失の見積りに特定の方法を要求していません。損失率法、ロールレート法、エイジング法、割引キャッシュフロー法、デフォルト確率（PD/LGD）法など、資産の性質に適した合理的な方法を選択できます。DCF法は選択肢の一つであり義務ではありません。銀行監督当局がUS GAAP上の見積方法を指定することもなく、公開営利企業とその他の企業で要求される方法が異なるという規定もありません。

questions.subtopic: AFS Debt Securities - Balance Sheet Carrying Amount
questions.stem: An entity holds an available-for-sale debt security on which it has recognized an allowance for credit losses under ASC 326-30. At what amount is the security presented on the balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Fair value

choices.label: B
choices.text: Amortized cost less the allowance for credit losses

choices.label: C
choices.text: The lower of amortized cost or fair value

choices.label: D
choices.text: Amortized cost, with the allowance disclosed only in the notes

questions.explanation: AFS debt securities are always carried at fair value on the balance sheet under ASC 320, regardless of any credit loss allowance. The allowance under ASC 326-30 does not change the balance sheet carrying amount; it determines how much of the decline below amortized cost is recognized in earnings (credit portion, via the allowance) versus in OCI (non-credit portion). Presenting amortized cost less the allowance describes the model for HTM securities and loans. Lower of cost or fair value applies to certain loans held for sale, not AFS securities.
questions.explanationJa: AFS負債証券はASC 320により、信用損失引当金の有無にかかわらず常に公正価値で貸借対照表に計上されます。ASC 326-30の引当金は貸借対照表の計上額を変えるものではなく、償却原価を下回る下落額のうち損益に計上する部分（信用部分・引当金経由）とOCIに計上する部分（非信用部分）の配分を決定するものです。「償却原価−引当金」はHTM証券や貸付金のモデル、「低価法」は売却目的保有の貸付金等に適用される処理です。

questions.stem: Under ASC 326-20, expected credit losses are measured on the basis of a financial asset's amortized cost. Which of the following is included in the amortized cost basis?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The asset's original contractual principal, ignoring any premium or discount

choices.label: B
choices.text: Unpaid principal adjusted for unamortized premiums or discounts, net deferred fees or costs, and previous write-offs

choices.label: C
choices.text: The asset's current fair value as of the measurement date

choices.label: D
choices.text: Unpaid principal plus all future contractual interest payments

questions.explanation: The amortized cost basis is the amount at which a financing receivable or investment is originated or acquired, adjusted for applicable accrued interest, accretion or amortization of premium, discount, and net deferred fees or costs, collection of cash, write-offs, foreign exchange, and fair value hedge accounting adjustments. Using only original principal ignores required adjustments for premiums, discounts, and deferred fees. Fair value is not the measurement basis for assets carried at amortized cost. Adding all future contractual interest would overstate the basis; future interest is part of contractual cash flows, not the current amortized cost.
questions.explanationJa: 償却原価とは、組成または取得時の金額に、未収利息（方針選択による）、プレミアム・ディスカウント・繰延手数料純額の償却、現金回収、償却（write-off）、為替、公正価値ヘッジ調整等を反映した金額です（ASC 326-20用語定義）。当初契約元本のみではプレミアム・ディスカウント・繰延手数料の調整を無視しています。公正価値は償却原価測定資産の測定基礎ではありません。将来の契約利息全額の加算は基礎を過大計上するもので、将来利息は契約キャッシュフローの一部であり現在の償却原価ではありません。

questions.stem: At contract inception, Vantage Corp. sells goods to a customer for $100,000 but, based on past practice, expects to accept $95,000 because it routinely grants price reductions to maintain the relationship. Vantage also estimates that some customers will fail to pay amounts due because of credit risk. How are these two items reflected in the financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Both reduce revenue as variable consideration

choices.label: B
choices.text: Both are recognized as credit loss expense under ASC 326

choices.label: C
choices.text: The expected price concession is credit loss expense; the customer nonpayment risk reduces revenue

choices.label: D
choices.text: The expected price concession reduces the transaction price as variable consideration under ASC 606; expected nonpayment due to credit risk is recognized as credit loss expense under ASC 326

questions.explanation: Anticipated price concessions that the entity expects to grant are a form of variable consideration that reduces the transaction price (and thus revenue) under ASC 606. In contrast, a customer's inability to pay due to credit risk is not variable consideration; the receivable is recorded at the full transaction price and an allowance for expected credit losses is recognized through credit loss expense under ASC 326. Treating both as revenue reductions ignores the credit loss model. Treating both as credit losses overstates the receivable and revenue. Reversing the two treatments misapplies both standards.
questions.explanationJa: 企業が許容すると見込む値引き（price concession）はASC 606の変動対価であり、取引価格（収益）を減額します。一方、顧客の信用リスクによる回収不能の見込みは変動対価ではなく、債権を取引価格の全額で計上したうえでASC 326に基づき期待信用損失の引当金を信用損失費用として認識します。両方を収益の減額とする処理は信用損失モデルを無視し、両方を信用損失とする処理は債権と収益を過大計上します。

questions.stem: Under ASC 326-20, over what period does an entity measure expected credit losses on a term loan?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The contractual term, extended for all modifications the lender expects to grant in the future

choices.label: B
choices.text: A fixed twelve-month horizon from the reporting date

choices.label: C
choices.text: The contractual term, adjusted for expected prepayments, but not extended for expected extensions or renewals unless the borrower holds a renewal option that is not unconditionally cancellable by the lender

choices.label: D
choices.text: The expected holding period determined by management's intent to sell the loan

questions.explanation: ASC 326-20-30-6 requires measurement over the contractual term, adjusted for expected prepayments. The contractual term is not extended for expected extensions, renewals, or modifications unless the entity reasonably expects to execute a troubled debt-type modification or the borrower holds an extension or renewal option (not unconditionally cancellable by the lender) embedded in the contract. Extending for all expected future modifications overstates the measurement period. A twelve-month horizon describes the IFRS 9 Stage 1 concept, not U.S. GAAP. Management's intent to sell does not define the CECL measurement period.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-30-6は、期待プリペイメントを調整した契約期間にわたって期待信用損失を測定することを求めています。契約期間は、貸手が無条件にキャンセルできない更新・延長オプションを借手が契約上保有している場合等を除き、見込まれる延長・更新・条件変更のために延長しません。すべての将来の条件変更を織り込む処理は測定期間の過大化です。12か月の測定はIFRS 9のステージ1の概念でありUS GAAPではありません。経営者の売却意図はCECLの測定期間を決定しません。

questions.stem: Under ASC 326-20-50, which of the following is a required disclosure related to nonaccrual financial assets?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The amortized cost basis of assets on nonaccrual status as of the beginning and end of the period, and the amount of nonaccrual assets with no related allowance

choices.label: B
choices.text: The fair value of all nonaccrual assets as of each interim date

choices.label: C
choices.text: The internal credit scores assigned to each nonaccrual borrower

choices.label: D
choices.text: A forecast of when each nonaccrual asset is expected to return to accrual status

questions.explanation: ASC 326-20-50-16 requires disclosure of the amortized cost basis of financial assets on nonaccrual status as of the beginning and end of the reporting period, the amortized cost basis of nonaccrual assets for which there is no related allowance for credit losses, and interest income recognized on nonaccrual assets during the period. Fair value of nonaccrual assets at interim dates, borrower-level internal credit scores, and forecasts of return to accrual status are not required disclosures.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-50-16は、期首・期末時点の未収利息不計上（nonaccrual）資産の償却原価、関連する引当金が設定されていない未収利息不計上資産の償却原価、および期中に未収利息不計上資産について認識した利息収益の開示を要求しています。中間期日ごとの公正価値、借手別の内部信用スコア、未収利息計上への復帰時期の予測は要求されていません。

questions.subtopic: Balance Sheet Presentation - Allowance as Contra-Asset
questions.stem: How should the allowance for credit losses on loans measured at amortized cost be presented on the balance sheet under ASC 326?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a separate liability in the liabilities section

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a valuation account deducted from the amortized cost basis of the loans

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a component of accumulated other comprehensive income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only in the notes to the financial statements, with loans presented gross

questions.explanation: ASC 326-20-45-1 requires the allowance for credit losses on financial assets measured at amortized cost to be presented as a valuation account (contra-asset) that is deducted from the amortized cost basis to present the net amount expected to be collected. Liability presentation applies only to expected credit losses on off-balance-sheet credit exposures such as unfunded commitments. The allowance is not an equity item within AOCI, and note-only disclosure with gross presentation does not satisfy the presentation requirement.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 326-20-45-1は、償却原価で測定する金融資産の信用損失引当金を、回収が見込まれる正味額を示すために償却原価から控除する評価勘定（資産の控除項目）として表示することを要求しています。負債としての表示は、未実行コミットメント等のオフバランス信用エクスポージャーに係る期待信用損失にのみ適用されます。引当金はAOCI（その他の包括利益累計額）の構成要素ではなく、注記のみの開示でグロス表示とすることも認められません。
